text: The effects of initial high-NO concentrations, varying relative humidity (RH) and aerosol acidity on secondary organic aerosol (SOA) formed from both ozonolysis and hydroxyl radical (OH)-initiated oxidation of α-pinene were investigated in the University of North Carolina dual outdoor smog chamber facility. SOA formation from ozonolysis of α-pinene under high-NO conditions was larger than that from OH-initiated oxidation. No significant effect of RH was observed. The O3-initiated oxidation of α-pinene in the presence of ammonium sulfate (AS) seed coated with SOA from OH-initiated oxidation of α-pinene resulted in less new particle formation compared to ozonolysis of α-pinene in the presence of pure AS seed. The UPLC/ESI-HR-Q-TOFMS analysis showed that the chemical components of α-pinene SOA include carboxylic acids and high molecular weight dimers. Among them, 8 types of carboxylic acids and 4 types of dimers were detected, accounting for 8–12% of the total mass of α-pinene SOA. Furthermore, the high-NO condition enhanced the formation of highly oxidized carboxylic acids such as 3-methyl-1,2,3-butanetricarboxylic acid (MBTCA) and diaterpenylic acid acetate (DTAA in DTAA) during the OH-initiated oxidation of α-pinene. However, the dimers were only detected in α-pinene SOA formed via ozonolysis in the absence of NOx. The yields of dimers were increased by a factor of two at higher RH (50–90%) relative to those at lower RH (30–50%). Increased aerosol acidity had little effect on the formation of these dimers. In addition, the SOA mass yield had little effect on the formation of carboxylic acids and dimers in α-pinene SOA formed via ozonolysis. Thus, the dimers were considered to be formed via gas-phase reactions of the sCI formed from α-pinene ozonolysis. The high molecular weight and polar nature of dimers formed in the gas phase may result in their increased particle number concentration as a result of homogenous nucleation. Indeed, three types of dimers (i.e. pinyl-diaterpenyl dimer (MW 358), pinyl-diaterebyl dimer (MW 344) and pinonyl-pinyl dimer (MW 368)) have been observed in both laboratory-generated and ambient fine organic aerosol samples, thus they can be used as tracers for the O3-initiated oxidation of α-pinene, and therefore can be used to reflect the degree of enhancement of anthropogenic activity. The homogenous nucleation of the high molecular weight and low volatility dimers resulted in the increase of particle number concentration.
